body:
## Summary

After updating to 2026.4.23 (commit steipete@3eb2a9d "fix(plugin-sdk): drop unavailable memory host exports"), `agents.defaults.memorySearch.provider: "gemini"` fails at runtime with:

```
[memory] sync failed (search): Error: Unknown memory embedding provider: gemini
```

## Environment
- OpenClaw version: 2026.4.23 (container, steipete@3eb2a9d + related memory-host changes)
- Config unchanged and valid according to schema:
  ```json
  "memorySearch": {
    "enabled": true,
    "provider": "gemini",
    "model": "gemini-embedding-001",
    "remote": { "apiKey": "***" }
  }
  ```
- The same config worked before 2026.4.23

## Root Cause
The commit cleaned up memory-host exports but appears to have removed the Gemini embedding provider registration from the runtime provider map. The config schema still lists "gemini" as a valid provider, but the embedding registry no longer recognizes it.

## Impact
- Memory search is broken for anyone using Gemini embeddings
- Workarounds: switch to openai/voyage/mistral temporarily, or disable memorySearch

## Expected Fix
Reinstate Gemini in the embedding provider registry (src/memory/embeddings or plugin-sdk) so config-spec and runtime behavior match again.

comments.body:
Thanks for the context here. I did a careful shell check against current `main`, and this is already implemented.

Current main already has the Gemini memory embedding path wired: the bundled Google plugin registers a `gemini` memory embedding adapter, its manifest declares the `memoryEmbeddingProviders` contract, and memory-core resolves providers through the config-aware runtime resolver before throwing the reported unknown-provider error. The named `3eb2a9d` shim cleanup was followed on main by `2ccdbc7d`, which restored the memory-host wildcard shims. I did not find a release tag containing that main-line state yet, so users on 2026.4.23 still need a newer build.

Best possible solution:

Keep the current main implementation and ship it in the next release; no further core change is needed unless someone can reproduce `provider: "gemini"` failing on a build that includes the current Google plugin registration and memory-host shim restoration.

What I checked:

- **Google plugin registers Gemini memory embeddings:** The bundled Google plugin calls `api.registerMemoryEmbeddingProvider(geminiMemoryEmbeddingProviderAdapter)`, and the adapter has id `gemini`, default model `gemini-embedding-001`, remote transport, and Gemini-specific multimodal support. ([`extensions/google/index.ts:156`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/e84ebeafbd67/extensions/google/index.ts#L156), [e84ebeafbd67](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/e84ebeafbd67))
- **Manifest declares provider ownership:** The Google plugin manifest declares `contracts.memoryEmbeddingProviders: ["gemini"]`, which lets the capability resolver load the owning bundled plugin for the Gemini embedding adapter. ([`extensions/google/openclaw.plugin.json:115`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/e84ebeafbd67/extensions/google/openclaw.plugin.json#L115), [e84ebeafbd67](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/e84ebeafbd67))
- **Runtime resolver is exported through the memory embedding SDK shim:** The current memory embedding SDK subpath exports `getMemoryEmbeddingProvider` and provider-list helpers from `memory-embedding-provider-runtime`, so memory-core uses the config-aware runtime/capability resolver rather than only a cold in-memory map. ([`src/plugin-sdk/memory-core-host-engine-embeddings.ts:2`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/e84ebeafbd67/src/plugin-sdk/memory-core-host-engine-embeddings.ts#L2), [e84ebeafbd67](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/e84ebeafbd67))
- **Unknown-provider error now sits behind config-aware lookup:** Memory-core only throws `Unknown memory embedding provider` after `getMemoryEmbeddingProvider(id, config)` fails. The resolver checks active registrations and falls back to bundled capability providers declared by manifests. ([`extensions/memory-core/src/memory/embeddings.ts:46`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/e84ebeafbd67/extensions/memory-core/src/memory/embeddings.ts#L46), [e84ebeafbd67](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/e84ebeafbd67))
- **Post-3eb shim restoration:** Commit `2ccdbc7d` restored wildcard exports for the memory host runtime shim after `3eb2a9d` removed unavailable exports, matching the regression window named in the report. ([`src/plugin-sdk/memory-core-host-runtime-core.ts:1`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/2ccdbc7dd93b/src/plugin-sdk/memory-core-host-runtime-core.ts#L1), [2ccdbc7dd93b](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/2ccdbc7dd93b))
- **Release check:** `git tag --contains 2ccdbc7d` returned no tags, while the latest tag in this checkout is `v2026.4.26`; the fix is on main but no containing release tag was found. ([e84ebeafbd67](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/e84ebeafbd67))

So I’m closing this as already implemented rather than keeping a duplicate issue open.

Codex review notes: model gpt-5.5, reasoning high; reviewed against [e84ebeafbd67](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/e84ebeafbd67a420faf22c516562c67dc66b8e7c); fix evidence: commit [2ccdbc7dd93b](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/2ccdbc7dd93bfcb08e100a479af79d8d8a211d97).

<!-- clawsweeper-review item=73527 -->

title: [Bug][Regression] memorySearch provider "gemini" unknown after steipete@3eb2a9d
